title: Introducing supa.page
excerpt: The marketing-site product I wanted while running Bonnard.
body:
Today we're launching supa.page — a hosted marketing-site product whose editor is **Claude Code in your terminal**.

## Why this exists

Every other marketing-site tool starts from "we have a browser canvas and a drag-and-drop UI". That's fine if you're a designer mid-flow. It's hell if you're a founder who wants to ship a landing page between meetings and never touch a UI again.

supa.page inverts the loop: the editor is the agent in your terminal. Typed objects for structure, Markdown for prose, plugin commands for everything else. No canvas, no chat UI, no log-in-and-poke. Edit, diff, publish.

## What this example demonstrates

This post is `featured: true`. A `post-feed` section configured with `featured: true` would surface it (and only other featured posts) at the top of the blog index.

Use sparingly — one or two featured posts per quarter, not "everything is featured".
